content.full_text:
Farn PoLrrrcAL PnacrrcEs CouMrssroN
428 J Street. Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
Jlurlre 24,2003
L.D. "Pete" Ross
2431 Spings Road
Vallejo, CA94591-5610
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-132
Dear Mr. Ross:
This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the campaign provisions of
the Political Reform Act.' Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(SXB), copy enclosed,
unless you are an aathoized representative of the Benicia Police Factlity/Civic Center
Improvement Project, for whom you seek guidance, the Fair Political Practices Commission
cannot address your questions or provide analysis. However, if you believe a violation of the
Political Reform Act has occurred, you have the option of filing a complaint with our
Enforcement Division. I have enclosed a fact sheet explaining the complaint process procedure.
Also enclosed for your review is our campaign manual on independent expenditure
committees.
Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
W. Wallace
General Counsel
Jwwjg
Enclosures
l. Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the California Code ofRegulations.

sections.conclusion: Unless you are an authorized representative of the Benicia Police Facility/Civic Center Improvement Project, for whom you seek guidance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. However, if you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you have the option of filing a complaint with our Enforcement Division.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide written advice on campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act regarding the Benicia Police Facility/Civic Center Improvement Project to a non-authorized representative?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide advice to the requester unless they are an authorized representative of the subject organization. The requester may file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if they believe a violation has occurred.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; extract confidence is high as the key language is clear, but minor uncertainty exists about whether this is technically a 'declination' subtype rather than general 'advice_letter' category.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can the FPPC provide written advice on campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act regarding the Benicia Police Facility/Civic Center Improvement Project to a non-authorized representative?

CONCLUSION: Unless you are an authorized representative of the Benicia Police Facility/Civic Center Improvement Project, for whom you seek guidance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. However, if you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you have the option of filing a complaint with our Enforcement Division.

Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(SXB), copy enclosed, unless you are an aathoized representative of the Benicia Police Factlity/Civic Center Improvement Project, for whom you seek guidance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. However, if you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you have the option of filing a complaint with our Enforcement Division. I have enclosed a fact sheet explaining the complaint process procedure. Also enclosed for your review is our campaign manual on independent expenditure committees. Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5660. Sincerely Luisa Menchaca W. Wallace General Counsel Jwwjg Enclosures l. Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109- 18997, of the California Code ofRegulations.
embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice on campaign provisions to L.D. 'Pete' Ross regarding the Benicia Police Facility/Civic Center Improvement Project, citing lack of authorization as a representative of that entity, but offers the option to file a complaint if a violation is suspected.
